message:
test(e2e): add desktop-side regression spec — scroll indicator must be position:absolute with mt:0 at md+

Companion to `_diag-scroll-indicator.spec.ts`. That spec exercises the
mobile-overlap fix at 320/360/375-width viewports, so it would NOT
catch a regression where the desktop treatment was silently broken.

Failure mode it closes: if someone later drops `md:absolute` or `md:mt-0`
from the indicator class string (easy typo when reordering class utilities
or removing responsive variants), the mobile suite still passes because
the flow-layout asserts nothing about position at md+. The article's
desktop layout would regress — the indicator would stack below the hero
content with a 48px margin instead of floating at the hero's bottom edge
— and nothing in CI would complain.

New spec renders each of the 8 articles at 1280×800 and asserts:
  • `getComputedStyle(indicator).position === "absolute"`
  • `getComputedStyle(indicator).marginTop === "0px"`

Mechanics mirror `_diag-scroll-indicator.spec.ts`:
  • same article/label map (8 entries)
  • `waitUntil: "load"` + 2500 ms settle to avoid barra-factor-model's
    never-idle network
  • `test.describe.configure({ mode: "serial" })` to prevent
    dev-server cold-compile flakes across parallel navigations
  • same "no hero" / "no indicator" error sentinels

Local run: 8/8 pass in ~2.6 min (Chromium, workers=1).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
